annotations.label: Block design

anchor.snippet: a block design is an incidence structure consisting of a set together with a family of subsets known as blocks

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general block-design / incidence-structure-with-blocks definition; combinatorial designs are absent.

annotations.label: Balanced design

anchor.snippet: A design is said to be balanced (up to t ) if all t -subsets of the original set occur in equally many

annotations.note: No notion of t-balanced block design exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Regular design (replication number)

anchor.snippet: each element occurs in the same number of blocks (the replication number , denoted r ) and the design is said to be regular

annotations.note: Replication number / regular block design is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Uniform (proper) block design

anchor.snippet: A block design in which all the blocks have the same size (usually denoted k ) is called uniform or proper

annotations.note: No uniform-blocks block-design predicate in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pairwise balanced designs (PBDs)

anchor.snippet: for t = 2 they are known in the literature under the general name pairwise balanced designs (PBDs)

annotations.note: Pairwise balanced designs are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Partially balanced design / PBIBD(n)

anchor.snippet: For t = 2 these are known as PBIBD( n ) designs , whose classes form an association scheme

annotations.note: PBIBDs / association schemes are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Incomplete design

anchor.snippet: Designs are usually said (or assumed) to be incomplete , meaning that the collection of blocks is not all possible k -subsets

annotations.note: Incomplete-design terminology is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Simple design

anchor.snippet: Designs without repeated blocks are called simple

annotations.note: No simple-design definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equireplicate design

anchor.snippet: a design in which each element occurs the same total number of times is called equireplicate

annotations.note: Equireplicate designs are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tactical configuration / 1-design

anchor.snippet: The simplest type of "balanced" design ( t = 1) is known as a tactical configuration or 1-design

annotations.note: Mathlib has an abstract `Configuration` notion of points/lines but not the design-theoretic tactical configuration / 1-design.

annotations.label: Binary matrix is incidence matrix of regular uniform design

anchor.snippet: Every binary matrix with constant row and column sums is the incidence matrix of a regular uniform block design

annotations.note: No block-design incidence-matrix correspondence is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 2-design / BIBD

anchor.section: Pairwise balanced uniform designs (2-designs or BIBDs)
anchor.snippet: we define a 2-design (or BIBD , standing for balanced incomplete block design) B to be a family of k -element subsets of X

annotations.note: Mathlib has no BIBD / 2-(v,k,λ)-design definition.

annotations.label: Order of a 2-design

anchor.section: Pairwise balanced uniform designs (2-designs or BIBDs)
anchor.snippet: The order of a 2-design is defined to be n = r − λ

annotations.note: Order of a 2-design (r − λ) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complement of a 2-design

anchor.section: Pairwise balanced uniform designs (2-designs or BIBDs)
anchor.snippet: The complement of a 2-design is obtained by replacing each block with its complement in the point set X

annotations.note: Complement of a 2-design construction is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fisher's inequality

anchor.section: Pairwise balanced uniform designs (2-designs or BIBDs)
anchor.snippet: Fisher's inequality , named after the statistician Ronald Fisher , is that b ≥ v in any 2-design

annotations.note: Fisher's inequality b ≥ v for 2-designs is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: No constant block-sum labelling

anchor.section: Pairwise balanced uniform designs (2-designs or BIBDs)
anchor.snippet: there is no choice of such a set which can make all block-sums (that is, sum of all points in a given block) constant

annotations.note: Block-sum labelling result for 2-designs has no formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unique (6,3,2)-design

anchor.snippet: The unique (6,3,2)-design ( v = 6, k = 3, λ = 2) has 10 blocks

annotations.note: The (6,3,2)-design is not constructed in Mathlib (BIBDs absent).

annotations.label: (8,4,3)-design

anchor.snippet: One of four nonisomorphic (8,4,3)-designs has 14 blocks with each element repeated 7 times

annotations.note: (8,4,3)-design example is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unique (7,3,1)-design (Fano plane)

anchor.snippet: The unique (7,3,1)-design is symmetric and has 7 blocks with each element repeated 3 times

annotations.note: No (7,3,1) Steiner-triple Fano-plane design construction in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symmetric design

anchor.snippet: The case of equality in Fisher's inequality, that is, a 2-design with an equal number of points and blocks, is called a symmetric design

annotations.note: Symmetric 2-designs (SBIBDs) are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Two distinct blocks meet in λ points

anchor.snippet: in a symmetric design every two distinct blocks meet in λ points

annotations.note: Pairwise intersection property of symmetric designs is not formalized.

annotations.label: Ryser's converse for symmetric designs

anchor.snippet: A theorem of Ryser provides the converse

annotations.note: Ryser's converse theorem for symmetric designs is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Finite projective plane as symmetric 2-design

anchor.snippet: Finite projective planes are symmetric 2-designs with λ = 1 and order n > 1

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Configuration.ProjectivePlane` axiomatises projective planes as configurations, not as symmetric 2-designs.

annotations.label: Fano plane (projective plane of order 2)

anchor.snippet: For n = 2 we get a projective plane of order 2, also called the Fano plane

annotations.note: No specific Fano-plane instance / construction is exposed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Projective planes exist for prime power orders

anchor.snippet: Projective planes are known to exist for all orders which are prime numbers or powers of primes

annotations.note: Mathlib supplies a `ProjectivePlane` instance for ℙ K (Fin 3 → K), giving prime-power-order planes via finite fields, but no general existence statement.

annotations.label: Biplane

anchor.snippet: A biplane or biplane geometry is a symmetric 2-design with λ = 2

annotations.note: Biplanes are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Order 0 biplane (digon)

anchor.snippet: The order 0 biplane has 2 points (and lines of size 2

annotations.note: Order-0 biplane example is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Order 1 biplane (tetrahedron)

anchor.snippet: The order 1 biplane has 4 points

annotations.note: Order-1 biplane / tetrahedral example is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Order 2 biplane (complement of Fano plane)

anchor.snippet: The order 2 biplane is the complement of the Fano plane

annotations.note: Order-2 biplane (complement of Fano plane) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Paley biplane (order 3)

anchor.snippet: The order 3 biplane has 11 points (and lines of size 5

annotations.note: Paley biplane is not constructed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Three biplanes of order 4

anchor.snippet: There are three biplanes of order 4

annotations.note: Classification of order-4 biplanes is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Four biplanes of order 7

anchor.snippet: There are four biplanes of order 7

annotations.note: Classification of order-7 biplanes is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Five biplanes of order 9

anchor.snippet: There are five biplanes of order 9

annotations.note: Classification of order-9 biplanes is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Two biplanes of order 11

anchor.snippet: Two biplanes are known of order 11

annotations.note: Order-11 biplanes are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Non-existence of biplanes of orders 5, 6, 8, 10

anchor.snippet: Biplanes of orders 5, 6, 8 and 10 do not exist, as shown by the Bruck-Ryser-Chowla theorem

annotations.note: Bruck–Ryser–Chowla and these biplane non-existence results are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hadamard matrix

anchor.snippet: An Hadamard matrix of size m is an m × m matrix H whose entries are ±1

annotations.note: `Matrix.IsHadamard` defines Hadamard matrices via `A * Aᴴ = n • 1`, specialising to ±1 entries over rings with trivial star.

annotations.label: Hadamard 2-design

anchor.snippet: The resulting 0–1 matrix M is the incidence matrix of a symmetric

annotations.note: Construction of Hadamard 2-designs from Hadamard matrices is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Resolvable 2-design

anchor.snippet: A resolvable 2-design is a BIBD whose blocks can be partitioned into sets (called parallel classes )

annotations.note: Resolvable designs / parallel classes are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bose's inequality for resolvable designs

anchor.snippet: If a 2-( v , k ,λ) resolvable design has c parallel classes, then b ≥ v + c − 1

annotations.note: Bose's inequality is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symmetric designs have no non-trivial resolution

anchor.snippet: a symmetric design can not have a non-trivial (more than one parallel class) resolution

annotations.note: No formalization of resolution-of-symmetric-designs result in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kirkman's schoolgirl problem as resolution

anchor.snippet: A solution of the famous 15 schoolgirl problem is a resolution of a 2-(15,3,1) design

annotations.note: Kirkman's schoolgirl problem / 2-(15,3,1) resolution is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: t-design

anchor.snippet: a t -design B is a class of k -element subsets of X , called blocks , such that every point x in X appears in exactly r blocks

annotations.note: General t-(v,k,λ)-designs are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: t-design is also s-design for s ≤ t

anchor.snippet: Any t -( v , k ,λ)-design is also an s -( v , k ,λ s )-design for any s with 1 ≤ s ≤ t

annotations.note: The s ≤ t derivation theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Steiner system

anchor.snippet: A t -( v , k ,1)-design is called a Steiner system

annotations.note: Steiner systems are not defined in Mathlib (no `Steiner` declarations found).

annotations.label: Derived design D_p

anchor.snippet: The derived design D p has point set X − { p } and as block set all the blocks of D which contain p with p removed

annotations.note: Derived designs are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Extension / extendable design

anchor.snippet: A design E is called an extension of D if E has a point p such that E p is isomorphic to D ; we call D extendable if it has an extension

annotations.note: Extendable designs are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Extension divisibility condition

anchor.snippet: If a t -( v , k , λ ) design has an extension, then k + 1 divides b ( v + 1)

annotations.note: The (k+1) ∣ b(v+1) divisibility condition is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Only extendable projective planes are orders 2 and 4

anchor.snippet: The only extendable projective planes (symmetric 2-( n 2 + n + 1, n + 1, 1) designs) are those of orders 2 and 4

annotations.note: Extendability classification of projective planes is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hadamard 2-designs are extendable

anchor.snippet: Every Hadamard 2-design is extendable (to an Hadamard 3-design )

annotations.note: Extension of Hadamard 2-designs to 3-designs is not formalized.

annotations.label: Extendable symmetric 2-design classification

anchor.snippet: If D , a symmetric 2-( v , k ,λ) design, is extendable, then one of the following holds

annotations.note: Classification of extendable symmetric 2-designs is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Finite inversive (Möbius) plane

anchor.snippet: A design with the parameters of the extension of an affine plane , i.e., a 3-( n 2 + 1, n + 1, 1) design, is called a finite inversive plane , or Möbius plane , of order n

annotations.note: Finite inversive / Möbius planes are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ovoid in PG(3,q)

anchor.snippet: An ovoid in PG(3, q ) is a set of q 2 + 1 points, no three collinear

annotations.note: Ovoids in projective 3-space are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Egglike inversive plane

anchor.snippet: Any inversive plane arising this way is called egglike

annotations.note: Egglike inversive planes are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Elliptic quadric ovoid

anchor.snippet: An example of an ovoid is the elliptic quadric , the set of zeros of the quadratic form

annotations.note: Elliptic-quadric ovoids in PG(3,q) are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Suzuki–Tits ovoid

anchor.snippet: If q is an odd power of 2, another type of ovoid is known – the Suzuki–Tits ovoid

annotations.note: Suzuki–Tits ovoid is not constructed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Classification of ovoids / inversive planes

anchor.snippet: If q is odd, then any ovoid is projectively equivalent to the elliptic quadric in a projective geometry PG(3, q )

annotations.note: Classification of ovoids in PG(3,q) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: n-class association scheme

anchor.snippet: An n -class association scheme consists of a set X of size v together with a partition S of X × X into n + 1 binary relations

annotations.note: Association schemes are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Commutative association scheme

anchor.snippet: An association scheme is commutative if

annotations.note: Commutative association schemes are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: PBIBD(n)

anchor.snippet: A partially balanced incomplete block design with n associate classes (PBIBD( n )) is a block design based on a v -set X

annotations.note: PBIBD(n) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: PBIBD determines an association scheme (converse false)

anchor.snippet: A PBIBD( n ) determines an association scheme but the converse is false

annotations.note: PBIBD/association-scheme correspondence is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: PBIBD(3) on A(3)

anchor.snippet: Let A (3) be the following association scheme with three associate classes on the set X

annotations.note: Concrete PBIBD(3) example is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: PBIBD(1) is a BIBD; PBIBD(2) with equal λ is a BIBD

anchor.snippet: A PBIBD(1) is a BIBD and a PBIBD(2) in which λ 1 = λ 2 is a BIBD

annotations.note: PBIBD-to-BIBD specialisation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sunscreen BIBD for skin cancer trial

anchor.snippet: Suppose that skin cancer researchers want to test three different sunscreens

annotations.note: Statistical sunscreen-trial BIBD example is not in Mathlib.
